steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage presents a structural characteristic of the series and then contrasts two opposing interpretations of it. The question asks which answer best describes the overall structure of the passage.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — To explain why many critics find the Aubrey/Maturin novels to be remarkably entertaining despite flaws in the novels' structures.: the passage presents competing views rather than building toward a single supported claim. ✗
  • Choice B — To present a reason the unusual structure that O'Brian uses for *Treason's Harbour* makes it one of his most complex books.: no solution is offered; the passage lays out a debate without resolving it. ✗
  • Choice C — To describe a characteristic of the Aubrey/Maturin novels and offer two differing viewpoints on this characteristic.: the passage names a structural feature of the series, then gives two contrasting interpretations — exactly this pattern. ✓
  • Choice D — To argue that the Aubrey/Maturin series should have the literary renown of *In Search of Lost Time*.: no judgment of superiority is made; the passage remains neutral between the two viewpoints. ✗
C is correct.
